initial_program:
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Columns that are completely filled with 5.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
all5Col(C) :- #count{ R : input(R, C, 5) } = N.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Guess the two all5 columns (the first Sep1 and the second Sep2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { Sep1 : all5Col(Sep1) } 1 :- Sep1 = 0.
choice 2 { Sep2 : all5Col(Sep2) } 1 :- Sep2 > Sep1.

% No other all5 column before Sep1, between Sep1 and Sep2, or after Sep2
:- all5Col(K), K < Sep1.
:- all5Col(K), Sep1 < K, K < Sep2.
:- all5Col(K), Sep2 < K.

% There must be a column left of Sep1 and a column right of Sep2
:- Sep1 > 0.
:- Sep2 > Sep1 + 1.
:- Sep2 < M-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Representative columns for the three zones
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
repLeft  = Sep1 - 2.
repMid   = Sep2 - 2.
repRight = Sep2 + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Middle row (integer division, the central row)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
midRow = N / 2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Guess the nearest nonzero rows above and below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { TopSrc : row(TopSrc), TopSrc < midRow,
             input(TopSrc, repLeft, V_TL), V_TL != 0, V_TL != 5 } 1.

choice 1 { BotSrc : row(BotSrc), BotSrc > midRow,
             input(BotSrc, repLeft, V_BL), V_BL != 0, V_BL != 5 } 1.

% Enforce TopSrc is the nearest above (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R < midRow, R < TopSrc.

% Enforce BotSrc is the nearest below (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R > midRow, R < BotSrc.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Offsets for the six sides (top & bottom)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
offset_top_left(VL)  :- input(TopSrc, repLeft,  VL), VL != 0,  VL != 5.
offset_top_mid(VM)   :- input(TopSrc, repMid,   VM), VM != 0,   VM != 5.
offset_top_right(VR) :- input(TopSrc, repRight, VR), VR != 0, VR != 5.

offset_bot_left(VLB) :- input(BotSrc, repLeft, VLB), VLB != 0, VLB != 5.
offset_bot_mid(VMB)  :- input(BotSrc, repMid,   VMB), VMB != 0, VMB != 5.
offset_bot_right(VRB) :- input(BotSrc, repRight, VRB), VRB != 0, VRB != 5.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Colour predicates for the six sides (they return a constant colour)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
left_top_color(C)  = 5 + VL :- offset_top_left(VL).
left_bot_color(C)  = 5 + VLB :- offset_bot_left(VLB).
mid_top_color(C)   = 5 + VM :- offset_top_mid(VM).
mid_bot_color(C)   = 5 + VMB :- offset_bot_mid(VMB).
right_top_color(C) = 5 + VR :- offset_top_right(VR).
right_bot_color(C) = 5 + VRB :- offset_bot_right(VRB).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Zone predicates (which columns belong to which zone)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
zone_left(C)  :- col(C), C < Sep1.
zone_mid(C)   :- col(C), C > Sep1, C < Sep2.
zone_right(C) :- col(C), C > Sep2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Output for the three zones, rows above the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C), R < midRow, left_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R < midRow, mid_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R < midRow, right_top_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Output for the three zones, rows below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C),  R > midRow, left_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R > midRow, mid_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R > midRow, right_bot_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. Central row  all cells are 5
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(midRow,C,5) :- output_cell(midRow,C), input(midRow,C,5).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12. Exactly one colour per cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Columns that are completely filled with 5.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
all5Col(C) :- #count{ R : input(R, C, 5) } = N.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Guess the two all5 columns (the first Sep1 and the second Sep2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { Sep1 : all5Col(Sep1) } 1 :- Sep1 = 0.
choice 2 { Sep2 : all5Col(Sep2) } 1 :- Sep2 > Sep1.

% No other all5 column before Sep1, between Sep1 and Sep2, or after Sep2
:- all5Col(K), K < Sep1.
:- all5Col(K), Sep1 < K, K < Sep2.
:- all5Col(K), Sep2 < K.

% There must be a column left of Sep1 and a column right of Sep2
:- Sep1 > 0.
:- Sep2 > Sep1 + 1.
:- Sep2 < M-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Representative columns for the three zones
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
repLeft  = Sep1 - 2.
repMid   = Sep2 - 2.
repRight = Sep2 + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Middle row (integer division, the central row)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
midRow = N / 2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Guess the nearest nonzero rows above and below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { TopSrc : row(TopSrc), TopSrc < midRow,
             input(TopSrc, repLeft, V_TL), V_TL != 0, V_TL != 5 } 1.

choice 1 { BotSrc : row(BotSrc), BotSrc > midRow,
             input(BotSrc, repLeft, V_BL), V_BL != 0, V_BL != 5 } 1.

% Enforce TopSrc is the nearest above (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R < midRow, R < TopSrc.

% Enforce BotSrc is the nearest below (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R > midRow, R < BotSrc.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Offsets for the six sides (top & bottom)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
offset_top_left(VL)  :- input(TopSrc, repLeft,  VL), VL != 0,  VL != 5.
offset_top_mid(VM)   :- input(TopSrc, repMid,   VM), VM != 0,   VM != 5.
offset_top_right(VR) :- input(TopSrc, repRight, VR), VR != 0, VR != 5.

offset_bot_left(VLB) :- input(BotSrc, repLeft, VLB), VLB != 0, VLB != 5.
offset_bot_mid(VMB)  :- input(BotSrc, repMid,   VMB), VMB != 0, VMB != 5.
offset_bot_right(VRB) :- input(BotSrc, repRight, VRB), VRB != 0, VRB != 5.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Colour predicates for the six sides (they return a constant colour)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
left_top_color(C)  = 5 + VL :- offset_top_left(VL).
left_bot_color(C)  = 5 + VLB :- offset_bot_left(VLB).
mid_top_color(C)   = 5 + VM :- offset_top_mid(VM).
mid_bot_color(C)   = 5 + VMB :- offset_bot_mid(VMB).
right_top_color(C) = 5 + VR :- offset_top_right(VR).
right_bot_color(C) = 5 + VRB :- offset_bot_right(VRB).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Zone predicates (which columns belong to which zone)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
zone_left(C)  :- col(C), C < Sep1.
zone_mid(C)   :- col(C), C > Sep1, C < Sep2.
zone_right(C) :- col(C), C > Sep2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Output for the three zones, rows above the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C), R < midRow, left_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R < midRow, mid_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R < midRow, right_top_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Output for the three zones, rows below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C),  R > midRow, left_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R > midRow, mid_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R > midRow, right_bot_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. Central row  all cells are 5
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(midRow,C,5) :- output_cell(midRow,C), input(midRow,C,5).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12. Exactly one colour per cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Columns that are completely filled with 5.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
all5Col(C) :- #count{ R : input(R, C, 5) } = N.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Guess the two all5 columns (the first Sep1 and the second Sep2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { Sep1 : all5Col(Sep1) } 1 :- Sep1 = 0.
choice 2 { Sep2 : all5Col(Sep2) } 1 :- Sep2 > Sep1.

% No other all5 column before Sep1, between Sep1 and Sep2, or after Sep2
:- all5Col(K), K < Sep1.
:- all5Col(K), Sep1 < K, K < Sep2.
:- all5Col(K), Sep2 < K.

% There must be a column left of Sep1 and a column right of Sep2
:- Sep1 > 0.
:- Sep2 > Sep1 + 1.
:- Sep2 < M-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Representative columns for the three zones
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
repLeft  = Sep1 - 2.
repMid   = Sep2 - 2.
repRight = Sep2 + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Middle row (integer division, the central row)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
midRow = N / 2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Guess the nearest nonzero rows above and below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { TopSrc : row(TopSrc), TopSrc < midRow,
             input(TopSrc, repLeft, V_TL), V_TL != 0, V_TL != 5 } 1.

choice 1 { BotSrc : row(BotSrc), BotSrc > midRow,
             input(BotSrc, repLeft, V_BL), V_BL != 0, V_BL != 5 } 1.

% Enforce TopSrc is the nearest above (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R < midRow, R < TopSrc.

% Enforce BotSrc is the nearest below (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R > midRow, R < BotSrc.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Offsets for the six sides (top & bottom)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
offset_top_left(VL)  :- input(TopSrc, repLeft,  VL), VL != 0,  VL != 5.
offset_top_mid(VM)   :- input(TopSrc, repMid,   VM), VM != 0,   VM != 5.
offset_top_right(VR) :- input(TopSrc, repRight, VR), VR != 0, VR != 5.

offset_bot_left(VLB) :- input(BotSrc, repLeft, VLB), VLB != 0, VLB != 5.
offset_bot_mid(VMB)  :- input(BotSrc, repMid,   VMB), VMB != 0, VMB != 5.
offset_bot_right(VRB) :- input(BotSrc, repRight, VRB), VRB != 0, VRB != 5.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Colour predicates for the six sides (they return a constant colour)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
left_top_color(C)  = 5 + VL :- offset_top_left(VL).
left_bot_color(C)  = 5 + VLB :- offset_bot_left(VLB).
mid_top_color(C)   = 5 + VM :- offset_top_mid(VM).
mid_bot_color(C)   = 5 + VMB :- offset_bot_mid(VMB).
right_top_color(C) = 5 + VR :- offset_top_right(VR).
right_bot_color(C) = 5 + VRB :- offset_bot_right(VRB).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Zone predicates (which columns belong to which zone)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
zone_left(C)  :- col(C), C < Sep1.
zone_mid(C)   :- col(C), C > Sep1, C < Sep2.
zone_right(C) :- col(C), C > Sep2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Output for the three zones, rows above the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C), R < midRow, left_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R < midRow, mid_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R < midRow, right_top_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Output for the three zones, rows below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C),  R > midRow, left_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R > midRow, mid_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R > midRow, right_bot_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. Central row  all cells are 5
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(midRow,C,5) :- output_cell(midRow,C), input(midRow,C,5).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12. Exactly one colour per cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

final_program:
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.
row(0..N-1) :- n_rows(N).
col(0..M-1) :- n_cols(M).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Columns that are completely filled with 5.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
all5Col(C) :- #count{ R : input(R, C, 5) } = N.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 2.  Guess the two all5 columns (the first Sep1 and the second Sep2)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { Sep1 : all5Col(Sep1) } 1 :- Sep1 = 0.
choice 2 { Sep2 : all5Col(Sep2) } 1 :- Sep2 > Sep1.

% No other all5 column before Sep1, between Sep1 and Sep2, or after Sep2
:- all5Col(K), K < Sep1.
:- all5Col(K), Sep1 < K, K < Sep2.
:- all5Col(K), Sep2 < K.

% There must be a column left of Sep1 and a column right of Sep2
:- Sep1 > 0.
:- Sep2 > Sep1 + 1.
:- Sep2 < M-1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 3.  Representative columns for the three zones
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
repLeft  = Sep1 - 2.
repMid   = Sep2 - 2.
repRight = Sep2 + 1.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 4.  Middle row (integer division, the central row)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
midRow = N / 2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 5.  Guess the nearest nonzero rows above and below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
choice 1 { TopSrc : row(TopSrc), TopSrc < midRow,
             input(TopSrc, repLeft, V_TL), V_TL != 0, V_TL != 5 } 1.

choice 1 { BotSrc : row(BotSrc), BotSrc > midRow,
             input(BotSrc, repLeft, V_BL), V_BL != 0, V_BL != 5 } 1.

% Enforce TopSrc is the nearest above (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R < midRow, R < TopSrc.

% Enforce BotSrc is the nearest below (no nearer row has a nonzero)
:- input(R, repLeft, V), V != 0, V != 5, R > midRow, R < BotSrc.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 6.  Offsets for the six sides (top & bottom)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
offset_top_left(VL)  :- input(TopSrc, repLeft,  VL), VL != 0,  VL != 5.
offset_top_mid(VM)   :- input(TopSrc, repMid,   VM), VM != 0,   VM != 5.
offset_top_right(VR) :- input(TopSrc, repRight, VR), VR != 0, VR != 5.

offset_bot_left(VLB) :- input(BotSrc, repLeft, VLB), VLB != 0, VLB != 5.
offset_bot_mid(VMB)  :- input(BotSrc, repMid,   VMB), VMB != 0, VMB != 5.
offset_bot_right(VRB) :- input(BotSrc, repRight, VRB), VRB != 0, VRB != 5.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 7.  Colour predicates for the six sides (they return a constant colour)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
left_top_color(C)  = 5 + VL :- offset_top_left(VL).
left_bot_color(C)  = 5 + VLB :- offset_bot_left(VLB).
mid_top_color(C)   = 5 + VM :- offset_top_mid(VM).
mid_bot_color(C)   = 5 + VMB :- offset_bot_mid(VMB).
right_top_color(C) = 5 + VR :- offset_top_right(VR).
right_bot_color(C) = 5 + VRB :- offset_bot_right(VRB).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 8.  Zone predicates (which columns belong to which zone)
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
zone_left(C)  :- col(C), C < Sep1.
zone_mid(C)   :- col(C), C > Sep1, C < Sep2.
zone_right(C) :- col(C), C > Sep2.

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 9.  Output for the three zones, rows above the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C), R < midRow, left_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R < midRow, mid_top_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R < midRow, right_top_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 10. Output for the three zones, rows below the middle row
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_left(C),  R > midRow, left_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_mid(C),  R > midRow, mid_bot_color(C).

output(R,C,Color) :- output_cell(R,C), zone_right(C), R > midRow, right_bot_color(C).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 11. Central row  all cells are 5
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
output(midRow,C,5) :- output_cell(midRow,C), input(midRow,C,5).

% -----------------------------------------------------------------
% 12. Exactly one colour per cell
% -----------------------------------------------------------------
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.
